titleEn: ePropelled launches new integrated propulsion unit for agricultural drones
titleZh: ePropelled推出新型农用无人机集成推进单元
summaryZh: 随着农业无人机需求快速增长，ePropelled公司推出了一款集成推进与动力技术的新型单元，旨在提升无人机性能。该产品通过优化动力系统，有望提高农业作业效率，但具体与智慧畜牧的直接关联尚不明确，更多适用于大田作物或植保场景。
translationZh: 农业无人机需求正在快速增长，推进其技术进步是占据重要市场份额的关键。一款集成了推进与动力技术的新型单元已投放市场，旨在提升无人机性能。
featuredReason: 该新闻展示了农业无人机动力系统的最新进展，虽不直接针对畜牧场景，但无人机在牧场监控、饲料喷洒等智慧畜牧环节有潜在应用，值得关注技术趋势。
contentFull: ePropelled launches new integrated propulsion unit for agricultural drones 03-06 | Drones | NewsePropelled has launched a new integrated propulsion and power unit specifically designed to improve spraying precision by agricultural drones. Photo: Chris McCulloughDemand for agricultural UAVs is growing at a fast pace and advancing their technology is key to capture significant market share. A new unit incorporating propulsion and power technology has been launched onto the market to improve drone performance.A new integrated propulsion and power unit specifically designed to improve spraying precision by agricultural drones has been launched by US company ePropelled. With a manufacturing plant based in New Hampshire, USA, ePropelled also has an innovation centre based in Coventry, UK, and a further engineering facility in Chennai, India. The company is experienced in developing electric propulsion and intelligent power units for uncrewed vehicles on land, air and sea, but its latest innovation is specifically targeting agricultural drones. GrowthWith a global market projected to exceed $4 billion by 2026, the agricultural drone sector is growing at between 20% and 30% per year. That growth is driven by demand for precision agriculture, maximising labour efficiency and adhering to government legislation on reducing chemical use. In order to do this, agricultural drones are becoming more precise in arable farming by enabling more targeted spraying, crop monitoring, and automated field operations. Therefore, tapping more into this market, ePropelled has come up with a new integrated system that combines high-efficiency brushless motors, advanced electronic speed controllers, and intelligent power management into a unified propulsion architecture designed to meet the unique demands of agricultural drone applications.Heavier loads, better precisionKnowing the market, ePropelled says agricultural UAVs require propulsion systems capable of delivering sustained high thrust, precise control, and reliable performance under challenging operating conditions, including exposure to dust, chemicals, and variable temperatures. The company says its system has been engineered to address these challenges by delivering high thrust density to support heavy payload spraying and spreading missions.The new unit delivers improved energy efficiency to extend flight time and reduce operating costs and has advanced thermal management for sustained performance in harsh environments. The integrated motor and ESC control offers more precise and consistent spray application.Overall, the result is a propulsion platform optimised for both light monitoring drones and heavy lift systems operating at scale across large agricultural environments.Reliable performanceDean Marcarelli, Chief Commercial Officer at ePropelled said: “With agricultural drones becoming a core part of farm operations globally, propulsion is no longer just a component decision, it is a mission critical enabler.” “OEMs and operators are under pressure to deliver higher productivity, reduce input costs, and operate more sustainably. By integrating propulsion, control, and intelligent power into a single optimised system, we are enabling a new generation of UAVs that can operate longer, carry more, and perform more reliably in real-world conditions.”Drone shiftePropelled recognises the UAV industry is shifting more towards fully integrated propulsion systems, where motors, control electronics, and power management are engineered together to maximise efficiency, reliability, and ease of integration. While initially developed for agricultural UAV applications, the same propulsion and power architecture can also be applied to a wider class of uncrewed systems, including ground-based robotic platforms used in precision agriculture and autonomous field operations.Dean Marcarelli added: “Over time, this same architecture will also support broader adoption of autonomous systems across both aerial and ground based platforms.”New ePropelled unit benefitsBy delivering a unified motor, ESC, and intelligent power architecture, ePropelled has a number of benefits for OEMs.Improved energy efficiency with extended flight timesReduced operating costs Advanced thermal management for sustained performance in harsh environmentsReduced system complexity and development timeImproved reliability and operational consistencyEnhanced real-time monitoring and diagnosticsSupports more advanced autonomous and data-driven applications Chris Mccullough Freelance multi-media journalistMore aboutautonomous technologydrone technologyprecision agricultureRelated articlesPremium30-06 | Photo reportDLG Feldtage: hot, dusty and relatively quiet25-06 | NewsAgreenculture acquires Chouette to add agronomic intelligence to autonomous machines25-06 | NewsFieldwork Robotics secures new funding to accelerate raspberry harvesting robotPremium24-06 | ArticleAustralian vegetable farm puts 2 weeding technologies to the test Buyers guides & Catalogues Subscribe to our YouTube channel Taiwan Smart Agriweek 2026Sep 8-10, 2026 TaiNEX 1, Tapei
author: Chris McCullough
contentHtml: Demand for agricultural UAVs is growing at a fast pace and advancing their technology is key to capture significant market share. A new unit incorporating propulsion and power technology has been launched onto the market to improve drone performance.
